questions.text: A pure substance consists of:
questions.explanation: Pure substances (elements or compounds) are made of only one kind of atoms or molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following is a homogeneous mixture?
questions.explanation: Homogeneous mixtures have a uniform composition throughout.

questions.text: A mixture of two or more metals, or a metal and a non-metal is called:
questions.explanation: Alloys like brass or steel are solid solutions of metals.

questions.text: The component of the solution that is dissolved in the solvent is called:
questions.explanation: Solute is the substance present in smaller quantity in a solution.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a gas in gas solution?
questions.explanation: Air is a homogeneous mixture of various gases like Nitrogen and Oxygen.

questions.text: Tincture of iodine is a solution of:
questions.explanation: Tincture of iodine uses iodine as solute and alcohol as solvent.

questions.text: The particle size in a true solution is less than:
questions.explanation: True solution particles are extremely small (< 10⁻⁹ m) and cannot be seen by naked eye.

questions.text: Which of the following shows the Tyndall effect?
questions.explanation: Tyndall effect (scattering of light) is shown by colloids like milk or fog.

questions.text: A suspension is a ________ mixture.
questions.explanation: Suspensions have visible particles that settle down when left undisturbed.

questions.text: In a colloidal solution, the substance distributed as particles is called:
questions.explanation: Colloids consist of a dispersed phase (particles) and a dispersion medium.

questions.text: Fog and clouds are examples of:
questions.explanation: Aerosols are colloids where liquid or solid is dispersed in gas.

questions.text: The process used to separate cream from milk is:
questions.explanation: Centrifugation forces denser particles to the bottom and lighter particles to the top.

questions.text: Two miscible liquids with a boiling point difference of more than 25 K are separated by:
questions.explanation: Simple distillation works when the boiling point gap is sufficiently large.

questions.text: To separate components of air, we use:
questions.explanation: Air is liquefied and then different gases are separated at different temperatures via fractional distillation.

questions.text: Crystallisation is better than evaporation because:
questions.explanation: In evaporation, some solids may char or decompose. Crystallisation yields purer solids.

questions.text: Which of the following is a physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting is a change of state; no new substance is formed.

questions.text: Who was the first scientist to use the term 'element'?
questions.explanation: Robert Boyle used the term in 1661.

questions.text: A substance that cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical reactions is:
questions.explanation: Elements are the basic form of matter that cannot be further simplified.

questions.text: Which of the following is a liquid metal at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature.

questions.text: Which of the following is a liquid non-metal at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Bromine is a non-metal that exists as a liquid at room temperature.

questions.text: Elements having properties intermediate between metals and non-metals are:
questions.explanation: Examples include Boron, Silicon, and Germanium.

questions.text: A compound differs from a mixture in that:
questions.explanation: In a compound, elements combine chemically in a fixed ratio and lose their original properties.

questions.text: Shaving cream is a:
questions.explanation: Foam is a colloid where gas is dispersed in liquid.

questions.text: Brass contains ________ percent of Zinc.
questions.explanation: Brass is an alloy of 70% Copper and 30% Zinc.

questions.text: A solution that has dissolved as much solute as it is capable of dissolving at a given temperature is:
questions.explanation: A saturated solution contains the maximum amount of solute at that temperature.

questions.text: The technique used to separate pigments from natural colors is:
questions.explanation: Chromatography separates components based on their solubility in a moving solvent.

questions.text: Camphor is separated from salt by:
questions.explanation: Camphor sublimes, leaving the salt behind.

questions.text: When a beam of light passes through a colloidal solution, it:
questions.explanation: Colloidal particles are large enough to scatter light (Tyndall effect).

questions.text: Butter is an example of ________ dispersed in ________.
questions.explanation: Butter is a gel (Liquid in Solid).

questions.text: Milk of magnesia is a:
questions.explanation: It is a suspension of magnesium hydroxide in water.

questions.text: Solubility of a solute usually ________ with an increase in temperature.
questions.explanation: Most solids dissolve better in hot solvents than cold ones.

questions.text: Concentration of a solution is defined as amount of solute per ________ of solution.
questions.explanation: It can be mass/mass%, mass/volume%, etc.

questions.text: Which of the following is a metalloid?
questions.explanation: Silicon has properties of both metals and non-metals.

questions.text: The property of metals by which they can be drawn into wires is:
questions.explanation: Ductility allows metals to be pulled into thin wires.

questions.text: Properties of a compound are ________ the properties of its constituent elements.
questions.explanation: For example, water (liquid, non-combustible) is made from H2 (explosive gas) and O2 (combustion supporter).

questions.text: To separate a mixture of oil and water, we use:
questions.explanation: Separating funnel is used for immiscible liquids based on density difference.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a colloidal solution?
questions.explanation: Glucose solution is a true solution.

questions.text: The major component of air is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up approximately 78% of air.

questions.text: Cooking of food is a ________ change.
questions.explanation: Chemical bonds are broken/formed, and new substances are produced.

questions.text: Blood and sea water are examples of:
questions.explanation: Both contain many different components and vary in composition.
